# Garari Maker

> Garari Maker is a free, open-source, browser-based tool by Sufi Engineering for designing roller-chain sprockets and involute spur gears and exporting CNC/laser-ready DXF and 3D-printable STL files. "Garari" (گراری) is the Urdu word for sprocket. No CAD software or sign-up is required.

## Key facts
- Price: free (open source, MIT license).
- Runs fully client-side in the browser; installable as an offline PWA.
- App URL: https://gararimaker.bysufi.com/app/
- Languages: English, Urdu, Arabic (with RTL); units in mm or inches; light/dark themes.

## Tools
- Sprocket designer: ANSI B29.1M tooth profiles for ANSI #25–#100 and ISO 606 / DIN 8187 metric 05B–20B chains (the metric "B" series also matches the British Standard). Supports bore, hub (single/double-sided), multi-strand, set-screw, keyway, bolt-circle mounting holes, and lightening cutouts (holes or spokes).
- Spur gear generator: standard involute spur gears in the ISO module system (module, teeth, pressure angle, bore).
- Drive calculator: two-sprocket ratio, output RPM, chain length in links, and center distance.

## Outputs
- Layered DXF for CNC/laser cutting (the keyway is exported as a single clean closed contour to avoid double-cutting).
- STL for 3D printing.
- PNG/SVG image export and a printable specification sheet.
- Batch nesting: multiple parts arranged on one DXF sheet.
- Live 2D SVG preview and an interactive 3D model; weight estimate across 9 materials (mild/stainless steel, aluminum 6061/7075, cast iron, brass, nylon/Delrin, Hardox).

## Maker
- Built by Sufi Engineering (https://sufi.engineering), which also manufactures custom sprockets and industrial parts. Designs can be sent from the app for fabrication via WhatsApp or email.
- Source: https://github.com/SufiEngineering/garari-maker
